Skills Navigator | NWEA

Help all kids learn with an easy-to-use, engaging classroom assessment tool

Discover classroom assessment that makes a real difference for students and teachers with Skills Navigator®. Northwest Evaluation Association™ (NWEA™) worked closely with educators to design this engaging tool that helps teachers close achievement gaps, use data to guide instruction, and support kids with diverse needs.

Designed for grades K – 8, Skills Navigator helps you easily accomplish four key tasks: identify the skills students are ready to learn, check evidence of skill mastery, monitor student progress toward mastery, and provide instructional resources to meet students’ specific needs—on, above, or below grade level.

Fill out the short form on the right to download the complete brochure.

Founded by educators nearly 40 years ago, Northwest Evaluation Association™ (NWEA™) is a global not-for-profit educational services organization known for our flagship interim assessment, Measures of Academic Progress® (MAP®). Educators trust our assessments, professional development offerings, and research to help advance all students along their optimal learning path.

© 2015 Northwest Evaluation Association™121 NW Everett St. Portland, OR, 97209 | 503-624-1951

# MAP Growth makes it possible

Accurate, precise, insights from MAP Growth help educators make the best instructional decisions for students. Learn more about the features and capabilities purpose-built to help teachers close gaps and improve outcomes.

## Designed with student growth in mind

Built on a stable scale, MAP Growth provides trustworthy insights from season to season and year over year. Educators have the consistent information they need to support every student, whether they’re working on, above, or below grade level.

Download Fact Sheet

## Interim assessment for growth

Grade Level Coverage

K–12 Subject dependent

Grade-level Independence

Measures performance of every student, whether on, above, or below grade level—even if standards change

MAP Growth for Science

Support STEM efforts with MAP Growth for Science, an interim assessment for grades 2-12. This one science assessment covers three key domains: life sciences, earth and space sciences, and physical sciences. Use the precise, real-time results to personalize instruction throughout the year for every student.

Common Core MAP Growth

Common Core MAP Growth data provides you with a clear picture of K–12 student growth term-to-term and year-over-year. Get insights into every student’s learning needs, while gaining valuable information about student progress with a common core assessment that measures real growth—even if you experience proficiency drops on your summative assessment.

Course-specific Math

Wondering if your students are growing in Algebra? Use these assessments up to 3x per year to measure student knowledge and growth within specific courses. Every license of MAP Growth includes tests for Algebra 1 and 2, Integrated Math 1, 2 and 3, and Geometry. By including pre-requisite content and adapting to each student’s learning level, these course-specific tests offer a personalized assessment experience that provides immediate insights.

## How Can I Help My Learners Leverage Their MAP Data? Using the Student Profile Report (8–10 min)

## Basic Use

As you get started, follow these tips.

Quick access: To jump straight to a specific student, open View Reports > MAP Reports, and use the Student Quick Search.

View prior test data: You can choose previous terms from the menu at top:

The default—Most Recent—means the most recent term with test data, which could differ for each subject. To alert you when the most recent score comes from a prior term, an asterisk appears next to the subject score.

Change student, class, or term rostered: There are various ways to switch to a different student:

Percentile colors: Wherever you see color coding, it indicates the percentile (a percentage-based ranking) of the achievement your student reached. It compares your student with students in the NWEA norms study from the same grade and with the same weeks of instruction between testing (as specified in your MAP preferences).

# About Us

About Us

As a team of former educators, we understand the challenges facing teachers and leaders and the difference the right resources and support can make in being able to deliver the kind of instruction that sparks learning for all students. We understand that the most useful and effective tools and guidance are grounded in both the latest pedagogical research and educator insights into what works in the classroom.

## How We Work

At Student Achievement Partners, we believe providing joyful, asset-based, high-quality instruction is the key to preparing all students to thrive academically and lead choice-filled lives. By supporting educators with concrete guidance and tools to deliver relevant, engaging grade-level instruction for every student, we can deliver on the promise of public education and unlock student potential

###### We Ground in Evidence

Our tools and recommendations are grounded in two kinds of evidence. We start with the research of what works for students and how educators can best support their learning. We also gather the perspectives of on-the-ground educators to ensure that we’re providing actionable, useful solutions to real-life challenges students and their teachers are facing.

###### We Move Boldly

Real change for students requires real action. With impact for students as our North Star, we’re committed to taking bold action to dismantle the disparities in who has access to relevant, engaging, grade-level instruction.

###### We Center STUDENTS

We focus on students in the design of our tools and frameworks through an equitable design approach that involves centering the experiences and perspectives of practitioners who both reflect their experiences personally and work every day with those students.

###### We are Responsive

We believe in lifelong learning and that there are always opportunities to improve. That’s why we’re always adapting to the latest research and the changing needs of students, educators, and our education system.

## Who We Are

Starting in 2011, Student Achievement Partners (historically known as Achieve the Core) began developing tools and resources designed to give more students access to high-quality, standards-aligned math and literacy instruction. You’re likely familiar with our tools and resources, which have been adopted by leading education organizations and millions of classroom teachers. Our standards-aligned classroom resources have been downloaded more than 37 million times.

# Research Report: "SAP MAP" in the Context of Education Assessment
## Focus: New York State 3rd Grade Testing and Standards Alignment

---

## Executive Summary

The phrase "SAP MAP" in education assessment can refer to the intersection of two distinct entities/concepts, or to a specific test-prep product line. After extensive research, the key findings are:

1. **SAP = Student Achievement Partners** -- a national nonprofit (learnwithsap.org, formerly "Achieve the Core") founded by the authors of the Common Core State Standards. SAP provides standards-aligned tools, frameworks, and resources for math and literacy instruction.

2. **MAP = Multiple possible meanings** depending on context:
   - **MAP Growth (NWEA)** -- the widely used adaptive interim assessment ("Measures of Academic Progress") by NWEA, used extensively in New York City and across NYS.
   - **MAP / MAP Plus (Continental Press)** -- "Mathematics Assessment Practice," a NY-specific test-prep workbook series (Grades 3-8) published by Continental Press, explicitly designed to mirror the New York State Math Test format and align to NY Next Generation Mathematics Learning Standards.

3. **NWEA produces the actual NY State Tests**: As of 2024, NWEA (a division of HMH) develops and publishes the New York State Testing Program (NYSTP) Grades 3-8 ELA and Mathematics tests under contract with NYSED.

4. **Linking studies connect MAP Growth RIT scores to NY state proficiency levels**: NWEA has published linking studies that allow educators to predict how students will perform on the NYSTP based on their MAP Growth RIT scores, including cut scores for Grade 3.

5. **Continental Press MAP Plus** is the product most literally called "MAP" in the NY Grade 3 test-prep context -- it is a standards-aligned practice test booklet specifically designed to prepare students for the NYS Math Test.

---

## Detailed Findings by Research Question

### 1. What does "SAP" stand for in education?

**Student Achievement Partners (SAP)** is a national nonprofit organization founded in 2011. It was historically known as "Achieve the Core" (achievethecore.org) before rebranding to learnwithsap.org.

**Key facts about SAP:**
- Founded by the experts who wrote the Common Core State Standards [LinkedIn, Student Achievement Partners profile]
- Composed of former K-12 educators and school leaders with deep expertise in literacy, mathematics, and curriculum development [LinkedIn, 2025]
- Provides tools including: Coherence Map (connecting Common Core Math Standards), Math Milestones (grade-level grids K-8), Instructional Practice Guide (IPG), Academic Word Finder, and the e2 Instructional Practice Suite [LearnWithSAP.org, About page]
- Resources have been downloaded more than 37 million times [LearnWithSAP.org, About page]
- Mission: "providing joyful, asset-based, high-quality instruction is the key to preparing all students to thrive academically and lead choice-filled lives" [LearnWithSAP.org, 2024]

**Source URLs:**
- https://www.learnwithsap.org/
- https://learnwithsap.org/about/
- https://cn.linkedin.com/company/student-achievement-partners

---

### 2. What does "MAP" stand for in education assessment?

**MAP has multiple meanings in education:**

#### a) NWEA MAP Growth ("Measures of Academic Progress")
- The most widely known "MAP" in education is **MAP Growth** by NWEA (Northwest Evaluation Association, now a division of HMH)
- Computer-adaptive interim assessment for K-12
- Measures student achievement and growth in Math, Reading, Language Usage, and Science
- Uses the RIT (Rasch Unit) scale, which is grade-independent and allows tracking of growth over time
- Administered up to 3 times per year (fall, winter, spring)
- Aligned to state standards, Common Core, and NGSS [NWEA MAP Growth Features page]

**Source URLs:**
- https://www.nwea.org/map-growth/
- https://www.nwea.org/map-growth/features/

#### b) Continental Press MAP / MAP Plus ("Mathematics Assessment Practice")
- Published by Continental Press
- Full product name: "New York State MAP Plus, Third Edition"
- Grades 3-8 test preparation workbooks
- Specifically designed to prepare students for the **New York State Math Test**
- Follows specifications from the NYS Testing Program Educator Guide
- Mirrors the NY State Math Test in: two sessions, tools and reference sheet use, calculator use, scoring framework, number and types of questions
- Two parallel forms (A and B) per grade for pre/post testing
- Teacher's Guides include standards correlations, scoring sheets, rubrics, and skill analysis charts
- Also sold alongside "ELAP Plus" (English Language Arts Assessment Practice) [Continental Press product page; Amazon listing]

**Source URLs:**
- https://www.continentalpress.com/product/new-york-state-map-plus-third-edition/
- https://www.amazon.com/State-Student-Booklets-Teacher-Continental/dp/B07MVKVR97
- https://iteacherschoice.com/educationb/continental3edmap

---

### 3. How does MAP relate to New York State assessments?

#### NWEA's dual role in New York

NWEA has a uniquely important position in New York State education:

**a) NWEA develops the actual NY State Tests:**
The 2024 NYS Testing Program Technical Report for Grades 3-8 ELA and Mathematics states it was "Developed and published under contract with the New York State Education Department by NWEA, a division of HMH" [NYSED Technical Report, 2024]. This means NWEA is the test development contractor for the official state summative assessment.

**b) NWEA also provides MAP Growth as an interim/benchmark assessment:**
New York City (the largest school district) uses MAP Growth as its universal interim assessment. The "MAP Growth Administration and Operations Guidance" for NYC covers School Year 2025-2026 [NWEA NYC Administration Guide, updated September 2025].

**c) Linking studies connect MAP Growth to NYSTP performance:**
NWEA published a formal linking study: "Predicting Performance on the New York State Testing Program (NYSTP) based on NWEA MAP Growth Scores" (July 2020, authored by Sarah Tran). This study provides **cut scores** that map MAP Growth RIT scores to the four NYSTP performance levels for each grade and subject [NWEA Linking Study, 2020].

An additional linking study was published in October 2024: "Predicting Proficiency on the New York State Regents Examination in Algebra I Based on NWEA MAP Growth Scores" [NWEA, 2024].

NWEA also provides "default cut scores" (from a generic linking study) for partners without a state-specific linking study, updated in August 2025 with the 2025 Norms [NWEA Default Cut Scores page].

**Source URLs:**
- https://www.nwea.org/uploads/2020/02/NY-MAP-Growth-Linking-Study-Report-2020-07-22.pdf
- https://www.nysed.gov/sites/default/files/programs/state-assessment/ei-ela-math-technical-report-2024.pdf
- https://www.nwea.org/uploads/NYC_MAPAdminOpsGuide-AOG_NWEA_Guide.pdf
- https://www.nwea.org/uploads/2024-NY-Regents-EOC-MAP-Growth-Linking-Study-Report.pdf
- https://www.nwea.org/resources/default-cut-scores-for-map-growth-reading-and-mathematics-assessments/

---

### 4. How does this map to NY state standards for 3rd grade?

#### NY Next Generation Learning Standards
New York State adopted the **Next Generation Mathematics Learning Standards** and **Next Generation English Language Arts Learning Standards**, which replaced the Common Core-branded standards (though the content is substantially similar). The NYSED provides Educator Guides for the Grades 3-8 tests that outline:
- Test specifications (sessions, question types, tools)
- Standards coverage
- Scoring frameworks and performance levels

The 2026 Educator Guides were last updated November 2025 [NYSED Educator Guide for Grades 3-8 Mathematics; NYSED Educator Guide for Grades 3-8 ELA].

**Source URLs:**
- https://www.nysed.gov/sites/default/files/programs/state-assessment/3-8-educator-guide-math.pdf
- https://www.nysed.gov/sites/default/files/programs/state-assessment/3-8-educator-guide-ela.pdf

#### MAP Growth alignment to NY standards
MAP Growth tests are aligned to state standards on an item-by-item basis. NWEA's blog describes how "the MAP Growth item pool stays deep, aligned, and up to date" [NWEA Blog, March 2025]. The MAP Growth Features page confirms alignment to "State standards, Common Core, Next Generation Science Standards (NGSS), AERO" [NWEA MAP Growth Features].

The NWEA New York state solutions page specifically states: "The MAP Suite of assessments can help New York teachers understand what their students know and are ready to learn next" [NWEA New York page].

**Source URLs:**
- https://www.nwea.org/blog/2025/how-the-map-growth-item-pool-stays-deep-aligned-and-up-to-date/
- https://www.nwea.org/map-growth/features/
- https://www.nwea.org/state-solutions/new-york/

#### SAP (Student Achievement Partners) tools for standards alignment
SAP provides several tools relevant to standards alignment:
- **Coherence Map**: Uncovers connections among Common Core Math Standards, showing prerequisite relationships
- **Math Milestones Grade-Level Grids**: Grade K-8 grids reflecting concepts, fluencies, applications, and practices at each grade level as defined by the standards. The Grade 3 grid is available for free download. "The reverse side of each grid includes Standards information for each task." [LearnWithSAP.org, Math Milestones page]
- **Focus in Mathematics**: Identifies the major work of each grade
- **Instructional Materials Evaluation Tool (IMET)**: Evaluates whether materials are aligned to standards

**Source URLs:**
- https://learnwithsap.org/math-milestones/grade-level-grids/
- https://tools.achievethecore.org/coherence-map/

---

### 5. How is this used for 3rd grade test preparation?

#### Continental Press MAP Plus (most direct "MAP" test prep for NY Grade 3)
- Grade 3 Student Booklets (Form A: ISBN 1498-8; Form B: ISBN 1504-6) at $6.00 each (minimum order 30)
- Class Sets include 30 student booklets + 1 Teacher's Guide at $181.50
- Teacher's Guide includes: directions with time suggestions, answer keys with standards correlations, scoring sheets, reproducible answer sheets, skill analysis charts, rubrics, and a "Connecting Instruction to Assessment" chart for remediation
- Mirrors the actual NYS Math Test format exactly [Continental Press product page]

#### NWEA MAP Growth for Grade 3
- 3rd grade is a key milestone year: "The NWEA MAP Growth Test for 3rd Grade marks a key milestone as students shift into deeper reading comprehension" [TestPrep-Online]
- Students in grades 1-8 are assessed in reading and math in fall, winter, and spring [Medinah District 11 Parent Guide]
- Practice tests are available at test.mapnwea.org [NWEA Practice Tests page]
- The Student Profile Report provides individual student data including RIT scores, percentiles, and growth metrics [NWEA Student Profile Report help page]
- Multiple third-party test prep resources exist (TestPrep-Online, Practice4You, GiftedReady, Teachers Pay Teachers)

**Source URLs:**
- https://www.continentalpress.com/product/new-york-state-map-plus-third-edition/
- https://www.testprep-online.com/map-3rd-grade-free-sample-questions
- https://practice4you.com/map-3rd-grade-free-practice-questions/
- https://teach.mapnwea.org/impl/maphelp/Content/Testing/PracticeTest.htm
- https://teach.mapnwea.org/impl/maphelp/Content/Data/SampleReports/StudentProfile.htm

---

### 6. Scoring and performance level mappings

#### NY State Test Performance Levels (Grades 3-8)
The NYSTP uses four performance levels:
- **Level 1**: Below Standard (NYS Next Generation Learning Standards not met)
- **Level 2**: Approaching Standard (partially meeting standards)
- **Level 3**: Meeting Standard (meeting grade-level standards)
- **Level 4**: Exceeding Standard (meeting standards with distinction)

Sample student score reports for 2025 are available from NYSED [NYSED 2025 Sample Score Reports page].

#### MAP Growth RIT to NY State Performance Level Mapping
The NWEA linking study (2020) provides **projected proficiency cut scores** that translate MAP Growth RIT scores into predicted NYSTP performance levels for each grade and subject. These cut scores allow schools to:
- Identify students at risk of not meeting state proficiency (Level 3)
- Set growth targets tied to state performance expectations
- Monitor interim progress toward state test readiness

The MAP Growth Goal Explorer tool (updated February 2026) helps with goal-setting by showing "a range of possible fall-to-spring growth goals" [NWEA Goal Explorer page].

Additionally, NWEA provides a **Learning Continuum** report that allows educators to "explore content on MAP Growth assessments" and see what skills students have mastered and what they are ready to learn next [NWEA Learning Continuum help page].

#### NWEA Skills Navigator (legacy product)
NWEA previously offered **Skills Navigator** for Grades K-8, which helped teachers: "identify the skills students are ready to learn, check evidence of skill mastery, monitor student progress toward mastery, and provide instructional resources to meet students' specific needs" [NWEA Skills Navigator page].

**Source URLs:**
- https://www.nwea.org/uploads/2020/02/NY-MAP-Growth-Linking-Study-Report-2020-07-22.pdf

- https://www.nwea.org/research/publication/map-growth-goal-explorer/
- https://teach.mapnwea.org/impl/maphelp/Content/Data/SampleReports/LearningContinuumRef.htm
- https://info.nwea.org/LP-Skills-Navigator-Dr.html

---

## Disambiguation: Which "SAP MAP" Did You Mean?

Given the ambiguity of "SAP MAP," here are the most likely interpretations:

| Interpretation | What It Is | Relevance to NY Grade 3 |
|---|---|---|
| **SAP (Student Achievement Partners) + MAP (NWEA MAP Growth)** | Two separate organizations/products: SAP provides standards-alignment tools; MAP Growth measures student growth | SAP's Coherence Map and Math Milestones help understand Grade 3 standards; MAP Growth provides interim RIT scores linked to NYSTP performance |
| **SAP (Student Achievement Partners) + MAP (Mathematics Assessment Practice by Continental Press)** | SAP provides the standards framework; Continental Press MAP Plus is a test-prep product aligned to those standards for NY | Continental Press MAP Plus Grade 3 directly prepares students for the NYS Grade 3 Math Test with standards-correlated content |
| **"SAP MAP" as a combined term** | No single product or program is officially named "SAP MAP" | The term likely refers informally to one of the combinations above |

---

## Limitations and Gaps

1. **No single product called "SAP MAP" was found.** The term appears to be informal or a combination of two separate education entities/products.
2. **The NWEA NY linking study PDF** (2020) could not be fully read in its raw form; specific Grade 3 RIT cut score numbers are contained within but were not extractable in this research session. The document is publicly available at the URL provided.
3. **Student Achievement Partners does not appear to have a direct formal partnership with NWEA** for MAP Growth specifically, though both organizations work in the standards-alignment space.
4. **Continental Press MAP Plus** is a commercial product with limited public documentation of its internal standards mapping.

---

# Research Report: "SAP MAP" in Education Assessment
## Focus: New York State 3rd Grade Testing and Standards Alignment

---

## Executive Summary -- 5 Key Findings

1. **SAP = Student Achievement Partners**, a national nonprofit (learnwithsap.org, formerly "Achieve the Core") founded by the authors of the Common Core State Standards. SAP provides standards-aligned tools, frameworks, and resources for K-8 math and literacy instruction.

2. **MAP has multiple meanings** depending on context: (a) **MAP Growth by NWEA** ("Measures of Academic Progress"), the widely used computer-adaptive interim assessment; and (b) **MAP Plus by Continental Press** ("Mathematics Assessment Practice"), a New York-specific Grades 3-8 test-prep workbook series.

3. **NWEA develops the actual NY State Tests** under contract with NYSED, while also providing MAP Growth as a separate interim/benchmark assessment used heavily in NYC and statewide. This creates a unique dual role.

4. **Linking studies connect MAP Growth RIT scores to NY state proficiency levels.** NWEA published a formal linking study (July 2020) that provides cut scores mapping MAP Growth RIT scores to the four NYSTP performance levels for Grade 3 and all other tested grades.

5. **No single product or program is officially named "SAP MAP."** The term most likely refers to either (a) Student Achievement Partners' standards-alignment tools used alongside NWEA MAP Growth data, or (b) the Continental Press "Mathematics Assessment Practice" (MAP Plus) workbooks for NY state test prep.

---

## Detailed Findings

### What SAP Stands For and What It Is

**Student Achievement Partners (SAP)** is a national nonprofit founded in 2011, historically known as "Achieve the Core." It was founded by the experts who wrote the Common Core State Standards and is composed of former K-12 educators and school leaders with deep expertise in literacy, mathematics, and curriculum development [LinkedIn, Student Achievement Partners profile]. Their resources have been downloaded more than 37 million times [LearnWithSAP.org, About page].

Key SAP tools include:
- **Coherence Map** -- uncovers prerequisite connections among Common Core Math Standards
- **Math Milestones Grade-Level Grids** -- K-8 grids reflecting concepts, fluencies, applications, and practices at each grade level, with standards information on the reverse side (Grade 3 grid is free)
- **Instructional Practice Guide (IPG)** -- observation tool for standards-aligned instruction
- **Instructional Materials Evaluation Tool (IMET)** -- evaluates whether curricula are standards-aligned

Sources:
- https://www.learnwithsap.org/
- https://learnwithsap.org/about/
- https://learnwithsap.org/math-milestones/grade-level-grids/
- https://tools.achievethecore.org/coherence-map/

---

### What MAP Means and How It Relates to State Assessments

**NWEA MAP Growth** ("Measures of Academic Progress") is a computer-adaptive interim assessment for K-12 covering Math, Reading, Language Usage, and Science. It uses the RIT (Rasch Unit) scale, which is grade-independent and allows tracking growth over time. Tests are aligned item-by-item to state standards, Common Core, and NGSS. Students typically test in fall, winter, and spring.

**Continental Press MAP Plus** ("Mathematics Assessment Practice") is a New York-specific test-prep workbook for Grades 3-8. The "Next Generation MAP" product mirrors the actual NYS Math Test in session structure, question types, calculator use, and scoring framework. It includes Teacher's Guides with standards correlations, rubrics, skill analysis charts, and remediation connections.

Sources:
- https://www.nwea.org/map-growth/features/
- https://www.continentalpress.com/product/new-york-state-map-plus-third-edition/

---

### How MAP Maps to NY State Standards

NWEA has a uniquely dual role in New York:

1. **NWEA develops the official NYSTP** (New York State Testing Program) for Grades 3-8 ELA and Mathematics under contract with NYSED. The 2024 Technical Report confirms: "Developed and published under contract with the New York State Education Department by NWEA, a division of HMH."

2. **NWEA provides MAP Growth as a separate interim assessment** used by NYC and districts statewide. NYC's MAP Growth Administration and Operations Guidance for 2025-2026 was last updated September 2025.

3. **The 2020 NY Linking Study** ("Predicting Performance on the New York State Testing Program based on NWEA MAP Growth Scores," authored by Sarah Tran) provides projected proficiency cut scores that translate MAP Growth RIT scores into predicted NYSTP performance levels for each grade and subject. This allows educators to use MAP Growth data to predict which of the four NYSTP performance levels (Below Standard, Approaching Standard, Meeting Standard, Exceeding Standard) a student is likely to reach.

Sources:
- https://www.nwea.org/uploads/2020/02/NY-MAP-Growth-Linking-Study-Report-2020-07-22.pdf
- https://www.nysed.gov/sites/default/files/programs/state-assessment/ei-ela-math-technical-report-2024.pdf
- https://www.nwea.org/uploads/NYC_MAPAdminOpsGuide-AOG_NWEA_Guide.pdf

---

### 3rd Grade Test Preparation and Alignment

**For MAP Growth (NWEA):**
- 3rd grade is described as "a key milestone as students shift into deeper reading comprehension"
- Practice tests are available at test.mapnwea.org
- The Student Profile Report provides individual RIT scores, percentiles, and growth data
- The Learning Continuum report shows skills mastered and skills ready to learn next
- Multiple third-party prep resources exist (TestPrep-Online, Practice4You, GiftedReady, Teachers Pay Teachers)

**For MAP Plus (Continental Press):**
- Grade 3 Student Booklets available in Form A (ISBN 1498-8) and Form B (ISBN 1504-6) at $6.00 each
- Class Sets (30 booklets + Teacher's Guide) at $181.50
- Directly mirrors the NYS Grade 3 Math Test format
- Teacher's Guide includes answer keys with standards correlations and "Connecting Instruction to Assessment" remediation charts

**For SAP (Student Achievement Partners):**
- Math Milestones Grade 3 grid provides formative tasks reflecting Grade 3 standards
- Coherence Map shows how Grade 3 math standards connect to prerequisite and successor standards
- These tools help teachers understand what students should know at Grade 3

Sources:
- https://www.continentalpress.com/product/new-york-state-map-plus-third-edition/
- https://www.testprep-online.com/map-3rd-grade-free-sample-questions
- https://teach.mapnwea.org/impl/maphelp/Content/Testing/PracticeTest.htm
- https://learnwithsap.org/math-milestones/grade-level-grids/

---

### Scoring and Performance Level Mappings

**NY State Test Performance Levels (Grades 3-8):**
- Level 1: Below Standard
- Level 2: Approaching Standard
- Level 3: Meeting Standard (proficient)
- Level 4: Exceeding Standard

**MAP Growth RIT to NY Performance Level mapping** is provided through:
- The 2020 NY Linking Study (state-specific cut scores)
- The generic default cut scores (updated August 2025 with 2025 Norms)
- The MAP Growth Goal Explorer tool (updated February 2026) which shows ranges of fall-to-spring growth goals

Sources:
- https://www.nwea.org/uploads/2020/02/NY-MAP-Growth-Linking-Study-Report-2020-07-22.pdf
- https://www.nwea.org/resources/default-cut-scores-for-map-growth-reading-and-mathematics-assessments/
- https://www.nwea.org/research/publication/map-growth-goal-explorer/
- https://www.nysed.gov/state-assessment/2025-grades-3-8-sample-student-score-reports

---

### Limitations

- No single product or program is officially named "SAP MAP." The term is informal.
- The specific Grade 3 RIT cut score numbers from the 2020 NY linking study are inside the PDF but were not fully extractable during this research. The document is publicly available at the URL provided.
- Student Achievement Partners does not appear to have a formal direct partnership with NWEA for MAP Growth, though both work in the standards-alignment ecosystem.
- Continental Press MAP Plus is a commercial product with limited publicly available detail on its internal item-to-standard mapping methodology.
